meta.name: The Kind Gods of Ilyon
meta.description: An ocean world must decide whether salvation remains a gift after its cures, food systems, and peace are revealed as the first layer of a planetary integration program.
meta.author: BigBirdReturns

attributes.name: Care
attributes.description: Keep concrete lives visible while institutions argue over worlds.

attributes.name: Evidence
attributes.description: Recover, test, preserve, and communicate contested knowledge.

attributes.name: Exteriority
attributes.description: Recognize and defend actors a governing model cannot own.

attributes.name: Systems
attributes.description: Understand infrastructure, dependencies, routes, and institutional power.

attributes.name: Resolve
attributes.description: Act before uncertainty can be made comfortable or complete.

progressionTiers.name: Arrival
progressionTiers.flavorText: The pocket experiences the controlling system as a concrete public good.

progressionTiers.name: Disclosure
progressionTiers.flavorText: Evidence changes what the rescue, cure, or governing order means.

progressionTiers.name: Refusal
progressionTiers.flavorText: The pocket acts before uncertainty can be made complete.

challenges.name: End the Fever
challenges.description: Keep the Benefactor cure moving through a city that cannot survive a constitutional argument at a child's bedside.

mechanicChecks.name: Triage the living
mechanicChecks.description: Care for the infected without reducing them to evidence in somebody else's case.

mechanicChecks.name: Hold the supply chain
mechanicChecks.description: Coordinate ports, clinics, and rival houses before the daily cure shipment spoils.

success.narrative: The fever wards hold. The cure remains real, and so does Ilyon's dependence on the hand that supplies it.

partial.narrative: Most receive treatment, but one district is missed. The argument now has names and graves.

failure.narrative: The chain breaks. Preventable deaths make every later refusal harder to distinguish from abandonment.

challenges.name: Map the Dependency
challenges.description: Trace which miracles can be locally maintained and which fail the moment the Benefactors withdraw.

mechanicChecks.name: Audit the gift
mechanicChecks.description: Follow medicine, harvest models, relays, and political mediation back to their indispensable nodes.

mechanicChecks.name: Keep it public
mechanicChecks.description: Publish the map without handing one local dynasty a replacement monopoly.

success.narrative: The dependency map is copied across the shoals. Salvation can no longer pretend it arrived without an ownership structure.

partial.narrative: The map exists, but key maintenance knowledge remains behind Benefactor seals.

failure.narrative: The audit becomes a purge. Clinics hide data, patrons close ranks, and the dependencies grow less visible.

challenges.name: Read the Dead Star
challenges.description: Prove what the neighboring ruins say without pretending Benefactor-built instruments are neutral witnesses.

mechanicChecks.name: Recover the sequence
mechanicChecks.description: Distinguish cure, integration, Crown, Answer, and ruin in the surviving record.

mechanicChecks.name: Record the contamination
mechanicChecks.description: Attach the intervention that made each fragment observable to the claim built from it.

success.narrative: The dead star yields a sequence, not a slogan. Its archive now travels with the history of how Ilyon recovered it.

partial.narrative: The lineage is clear, but motive remains disputed. Every faction can quote a different missing passage.

failure.narrative: A damaged symbol is promoted into certainty. The accusation outruns the evidence that was meant to constrain it.

challenges.name: Hear the Ocean
challenges.description: Ask what the planetary model excluded, without manufacturing a single convenient voice for a many-bodied ecology.

mechanicChecks.name: Translate refusal
mechanicChecks.description: Separate strategic response from migration, predation, and projection by frightened humans.

mechanicChecks.name: Refuse one Answer
mechanicChecks.description: Create political standing for incompatible ecologies instead of declaring one planetary sovereign.

success.narrative: The ocean enters the council as several actors capable of refusal. No one gets the simplicity of one planetary will.

partial.narrative: One reef nation is heard; others reject its standing. Representation becomes the next field of conflict.

failure.narrative: The translation collapses many ecologies into one hostile Answer and teaches the Benefactors exactly what to target.

challenges.name: Refuse Integration
challenges.description: Stop planetary closure without turning withdrawal into a sentence of famine, fever, and war.

mechanicChecks.name: Fork the systems
mechanicChecks.description: Split indispensable services before any party can use their withdrawal as command.

mechanicChecks.name: Hold the cost
mechanicChecks.description: Keep clinics and granaries operating while the integration mandate is refused.

success.narrative: Integration stops, but the gifts do not vanish. Ilyon inherits an expensive federation of systems no single actor can close.

partial.narrative: The Crown is delayed. Two regions retain independent systems while the rest remain inside the Benefactor mesh.

failure.narrative: The refusal is made without a viable substitute. Suffering restores the Benefactors' authority faster than argument can challenge it.

challenges.name: Carry the Evidence
challenges.description: Decide what leaves Ilyon, who may certify it, and whether exposing the pocket protects the exception or destroys it.

mechanicChecks.name: Bind the receipts
mechanicChecks.description: Package the claim, provenance, dissent, and uncertainty so no archive can claim more than it proves.

mechanicChecks.name: Choose the audience
mechanicChecks.description: Open the route knowing every recipient will convert the evidence into a different mandate.

success.narrative: The evidence leaves with its limits intact. Ilyon survives as neither laboratory nor sealed exception, but as a pocket whose refusal can be tested.

partial.narrative: The archive escapes through one faction's relay. The truth survives, already entangled with the institution that carried it.

failure.narrative: The route opens before custody is agreed. Ilyon becomes a prize in the argument its evidence was meant to discipline.

narrativeEvents.title: The pocket remains open
narrativeEvents.text: The immediate decision is recorded. Who possesses the authority to refuse salvation on behalf of people who will suffer without it? The answer now belongs to whoever inherits the changed map.
rewards: The cure network becomes a negotiated dependency
The route to the dead star is public

opening.narrativeText: Who possesses the authority to refuse salvation on behalf of people who will suffer without it?

options.label: Accept the indispensable gift
options.description: Preserve the concrete lives already dependent on the controlling system while entering its ledger.

options.label: Preserve the right to refuse
options.description: Keep the pocket outside closure, accepting the immediate uncertainty and cost.

organization.name: The Kind Gods of Ilyon Coalition

identity.title: The Kind Gods of Ilyon
identity.description: An ocean world must decide whether salvation remains a gift after its cures, food systems, and peace are revealed as the first layer of a planetary integration program.
identity.author: BigBirdReturns
parentCanons: The Godscar Codex, Book I: The Open Universe · first recension
The Godscar Codex, Second Recension Addenda · Book I: The Consequence Plane

godscar.pocket@1.controlQuestion: Who possesses the authority to refuse salvation on behalf of people who will suffer without it?

pressures.label: Ilyon
pressures.description: An oceanic world of rival thalassocracies, early astronomy, epidemic fever, famine, and dynastic war.

pressures.label: Final Humanity Benefactors
pressures.description: Offworld patrons end epidemics, stabilize food production, translate rival languages, and make their systems indispensable.

pressures.label: The uncommanded ocean
pressures.description: Migratory reefs, deep ecologies, and nonhuman intelligence are omitted from the planetary model because they cannot be made legible without being changed.

pressures.label: Planetary integration
pressures.description: The Benefactors are ready to connect medicine, harvests, navigation, and governance into one interoperable order.

pressures.label: The return of preventable suffering
pressures.description: Refusal risks renewed fever, failed harvests, fractured diplomacy, and deaths the Benefactors can prevent today.

pressures.label: The dead neighboring star
pressures.description: Orbital ruins record another uplift whose marine biosphere was deliberately Crowned, producing a system-scale Answer.

evidence.claim: The Benefactor mission is not merely humanitarian aid; it is preparing Ilyon as a deliberate Crown and Cascade laboratory.
evidence.venue: The Confluence of Tides, Ilyon's first intercontinental astronomical council
evidence.legitimacyTarget: The Benefactors' mandate to integrate the planet without informed local consent
evidence.upsideIfAccepted: Ilyon can negotiate, refuse, fork, or locally own the systems on which its people now depend.
evidence.downsideIfAccepted: Public trust can collapse before local institutions can replace offworld medicine, food stabilization, and conflict mediation.
evidence.failureIfFalse: A mistaken accusation destroys the only systems preventing epidemic death and renewed war.

receipts.label: The cure ledger
receipts.intervention: The Benefactors supplied the diagnostic standard and every surviving treatment chain.
receipts.limits: It proves lives saved and dependency created, not the patrons' terminal intention.

receipts.label: The dead-star geometry
receipts.source: Telescope spectra and recovered orbital inscriptions
receipts.intervention: Ilyon found the ruins using Benefactor-derived instruments and mathematical notation.
receipts.limits: The symbols establish lineage and sequence; damaged records leave motive contested.

receipts.label: Deep-tide testimony
receipts.intervention: Translation requires instruments that alter the acoustic habitat they observe.
receipts.limits: It demonstrates strategic refusal but cannot establish one unified planetary speaker.

factionReceipts.publicGood: They cure epidemic fever, stabilize food, and end wars that local powers failed to stop.
factionReceipts.characteristicFailure: Their integrated gifts narrow the future into a Crown experiment whose refusal becomes mass suffering.

factionReceipts.publicGood: They preserve independent infrastructure and the standing of actors that cannot survive total observability.
factionReceipts.characteristicFailure: Their demanded redundancy is slower, costlier, and can obstruct emergency care.

factionReceipts.publicGood: They keep food, law, kinship, and rescue networks operating across the inhabited shoals.
factionReceipts.characteristicFailure: They may use sovereignty as a reason to preserve hierarchy, war, and preventable disease.

cast.name: Aster Neral
cast.description: A local astronomer who recognizes Benefactor geometry in the ruins of the dead neighboring star.

cast.name: Talan Rook
cast.description: A fever physician whose patients live because the offworld cure still arrives each dawn.

cast.name: Iri Sable
cast.description: Speaker for the Free Observatory, buying time to verify the claim before dynasties or patrons weaponize it.

cast.name: Cael Arvon
cast.description: A reef-listener who can distinguish ordinary migration from the deep ocean's strategic refusal.

cast.name: Nacre of Deep Tide
cast.description: A temporary human-shaped organ through which the unmanaged ocean learns the model being imposed upon it.

consequences.label: The cure network becomes a negotiated dependency
consequences.description: Medicine remains, but no single patron may own every diagnostic, supply chain, and patient record.
consequences.inheritedBy: Ilyon's physicians and every child whose treatment depends on the settlement

consequences.label: The dependency map
consequences.description: Every indispensable Benefactor service is published with its failure mode, owner, and possible local substitute.
consequences.inheritedBy: The Confluence of Tides and future Crown auditors

consequences.label: Custody of the dead-star archive
consequences.description: The evidence survives outside both Benefactor and dynastic control, with its contamination history attached.
consequences.inheritedBy: The Free Observatory and Continuance witnesses

consequences.label: The ocean enters politics
consequences.description: Deep ecologies gain standing without being collapsed into one administratively convenient species or voice.
consequences.inheritedBy: Every coastal polity and the next generation of reef-listeners

consequences.label: Ilyon's systems remain forkable
consequences.description: Food, navigation, and medicine split into interoperable but independently refusible systems.
consequences.inheritedBy: Local guilds, rival cities, nonhuman nations, and offworld maintainers

consequences.label: The route to the dead star is public
consequences.description: The wider universe can now reach the evidence, and the evidence can attract every faction whose legitimacy it threatens.
consequences.inheritedBy: Ilyon and every power approaching through the reopened route

notes.foundation: The Godscar Codex, Book I: The Open Universe
notes.intent: Reference cartridge demonstrating the six-pressure Story Engine, disciplined canon uncertainty, faction receipts, and persistent consequences.

scales.operationalQuestion: What changed inside the immediate world, vessel, Tomb, or institution?
scales.authorityBoundary: The local receipt remains authoritative about local state.
scales.transitionTrigger: A bounded consequence changes a resident, dependency, archive, route, doctrine, or capacity.

scales.operationalQuestion: How did access, migration, quarantine, traffic, trust, or exposure change between pockets?
scales.authorityBoundary: Route actors may interpret contact but may not rewrite the source receipt.
scales.transitionTrigger: A local change alters who or what can cross a boundary.

scales.operationalQuestion: How do several routes and claimants reinterpret the bounded event?
scales.authorityBoundary: Sector standing records faction claims separately from source evidence.
scales.transitionTrigger: Several pockets, routes, or factions begin acting on the same receipt.

scales.operationalQuestion: Does the event accelerate, delay, redirect, conceal, or reveal a civilizational transition?
scales.authorityBoundary: Cascade classification remains a contested interpretation rather than automatic omniscience.
scales.transitionTrigger: The mechanism of change alters Crowning, Counterform, Answer, or large-scale convergence conditions.

changedPockets: Ilyon retains forkable medicine, food, navigation, and political systems.
changedRoutes: The route to the dead neighboring star becomes public and politically dangerous.
classificationShifts: The unmanaged ocean changes from environmental remainder to several actors capable of refusal.
Benefactor aid changes from neutral salvation to an indispensable system with an ownership structure.
factionStanding: Final Humanity retains credit for lives saved while its integration mandate loses presumed legitimacy.
The Uncrowned Compact gains standing as a redundancy and exteriority claimant.
Local dynasties retain sovereignty claims but must disclose the dependencies hidden inside them.
dissent: No single reef nation speaks for the whole ocean.
uncertainty: The dead-star archive establishes sequence more securely than motive.
sectorLedger.nextPressure: Every faction approaching through the reopened route can now contest custody of the evidence and the pace of Ilyon's integration.

authorityLimits: Aid does not grant integration authority.
Local sovereignty does not erase nonhuman nations or dependency receipts.
Evidence recovery does not grant one observatory ownership of the sector narrative.
legitimacyAfterContact.review: The Confluence of Tides reopens the mandate whenever a dependency, route, or claimant changes.

greatformLatency.observer: Any Greatform or sector institution receiving the dead-star and ocean receipts
greatformLatency.noticeInterval: Longer than the immediate Ilyon crisis and potentially longer than several local administrations
greatformLatency.localAuthority: Ilyon may act on bounded evidence before a slow observer completes a universal classification.
greatformLatency.antiCapture: No slow observer may use delayed recognition to retroactively erase local decisions or declare itself the original author.

prohibited: inventing an outcome in the receiving book
normalizing unfamiliar people into local vocabulary
claiming a map or authority the source did not grant
